what_it_does: This file is a YAML-frontmatter ledger entry that records an image attachment in the design-assets channel, pointing to the icon asset at image://design-assets/icons/Icon_Settings.
observations: Only YAML frontmatter is present; there is no body content.
The target field uses an image:// URI scheme.
It includes an id, channel, and an ISO 8601 added timestamp.
No code symbols or executable content are present.
standouts: The attachment targets a settings icon asset via image://design-assets/icons/Icon_Settings.
It is typed as an images attachment in the design-assets channel.
It functions purely as a metadata stub for a channel attachment.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Uses id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone offset.
Uses a URI-style target with an image:// scheme.
notes: A metadata stub that registers a settings-icon image attachment in the design-assets channel with a timestamped reference.
questions: What image format or resolution does the target asset have?
Where and how is the image:// URI resolved?
Who added this attachment and what change prompted it?
Is the target URI a canonical, versioned, or mutable reference?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record a channel attachment, and the file does exactly that.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a traceable, timestamped record that associates the design-assets channel with a specific settings icon image asset.
novelty: It uses an image:// URI scheme to address a channel-local icon asset.
contribution: It contributes a machine-readable attachment record that maps a channel to a specific icon image asset and its creation time, which a fused system could use to locate or index design assets.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter ledger entry for an image attachment in the design-assets channel pointing to the settings icon at image://design-assets/icons/Icon_Settings.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to register and timestamp a settings icon image attachment within the design-assets channel.
